path.name: Youth Systems Programming
path.description: A live youth programming course where students learn to architect and build scalable software systems by coding a Screeps colony with Git, APIs, automation, and AI-assisted development.
path.targetAudience: Schools, churches, nonprofits, workforce programs, and families who want students to build durable programming confidence through a structured, project-based environment.

programs.name: AI Systems Programming Lab
programs.description: Students learn programming by building a working Screeps colony system from the ground up. Across 6 weeks, they practice variables, functions, logic, Git, API thinking, automation, debugging, and Codex-supported development while seeing their code run inside a living strategy world.
programs.learningOutcomes: Understand variables, functions, conditionals, loops, data, APIs, Git commits, debugging, automation workflows, scalable system design, and how to use Codex as a coding partner while building in Screeps.
programs.projectSummary: A student-built Screeps bot that can harvest energy, spawn creeps, assign roles, persist memory, recover from failures, use Git history, and explain the architecture behind the colony.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A student-owned Git repository with Screeps bot code, commits, README notes, screenshots or demo clips, architecture notes, and a final reflection on how the system scales.
programs.liveSchedule: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern
programs.cohortNote: New cohorts run every so often. The next cohort opens Monday, August 3, 2026. Live sessions begin Tuesday, August 4, 2026.

sessions.name: Session 1: Screeps Setup and System Map
sessions.objectives: Set up Screeps, connect the development workflow, and understand the colony as a software system with inputs, decisions, outputs, and feedback.
sessions.liveActivity: Create the Screeps account or local environment, join the cohort server, create the first room, place the spawn, and map sources, controller, creeps, and memory.
sessions.homework: Capture a room screenshot and write what the spawn, source, creep, controller, and game loop do in the system.

sessions.name: Session 2: Variables, Values, and Colony State
sessions.objectives: Learn how variables store colony information and how state helps the bot remember what is happening each tick.
sessions.liveActivity: Write the first JavaScript controlling a creep, store room and creep values, and watch how changes affect harvesting behavior.
sessions.homework: Add three meaningful variables to the Screeps bot and explain what each one controls in the colony.

sessions.name: Session 3: Functions for Repeatable Creep Behavior
sessions.objectives: Learn how functions package repeatable behavior so the colony code becomes easier to scale.
sessions.liveActivity: Refactor harvesting, transferring, and upgrading actions into named functions and use Codex to suggest improvements students can read and explain.
sessions.homework: Create two Screeps functions and write plain-English notes describing their inputs, behavior, and outputs.

sessions.name: Session 4: Logic, Branches, and Room Decisions
sessions.objectives: Use conditionals, comparisons, and simple validation so the colony can make decisions.
sessions.liveActivity: Build if/else flows for empty creep storage, full creep storage, available energy, spawn capacity, and controller upgrades.
sessions.homework: Add one Screeps decision rule and document what should happen when the rule passes or fails.

sessions.name: Session 5: Git Checkpoints for a Living Bot
sessions.objectives: Use Git to save bot progress, compare changes, write useful commits, and recover from broken colony behavior.
sessions.liveActivity: Initialize or update the Screeps bot repo, make clean commits, review diffs, and connect version control to real software teamwork.
sessions.homework: Push the Screeps repo and write a README section explaining what the bot can currently do.

sessions.name: Session 6: Debugging a Running Colony
sessions.objectives: Learn how to inspect errors, logs, stuck creeps, idle spawns, broken memory, and unclear AI suggestions before changing code.
sessions.liveActivity: Diagnose real Screeps failures and practice turning room observations into precise Codex prompts.
sessions.homework: Open one repo issue describing a Screeps bug, the evidence, and the next fix to try.

sessions.name: Session 7: APIs, Game Objects, and Interfaces
sessions.objectives: Understand APIs by reading Screeps game objects, method calls, JSON-like data, and the interface between student code and the game world.
sessions.liveActivity: Inspect creep, room, source, controller, and spawn objects, then model how external APIs use similar request and response patterns.
sessions.homework: Document one Screeps object or method as if it were an API: what it receives, returns, and changes.

sessions.name: Session 8: Memory, Data Flow, and Roles
sessions.objectives: Organize data so the colony can track creep roles, tasks, energy needs, and room priorities.
sessions.liveActivity: Design simple Memory structures and connect harvester, upgrader, and builder roles to the functions and rules already built.
sessions.homework: Add or revise one Screeps Memory structure and explain why it helps the colony scale.

sessions.name: Session 9: Automation Loops and Scaling
sessions.objectives: Learn how automation uses triggers, rules, and repeated actions to reduce manual work and scale the colony.
sessions.liveActivity: Build spawn automation, role balancing, and simple repair/build priorities while discussing where automation helps and fails.
sessions.homework: Add one Screeps automation rule and note what should trigger it.

sessions.name: Session 10: Codex as a Screeps Build Partner
sessions.objectives: Use Codex to plan Screeps features, generate code, explain errors, and review work while keeping student understanding first.
sessions.liveActivity: Run a guided Codex workflow: describe a bot improvement, inspect the generated code, test in Screeps, and commit only understood work.
sessions.homework: Use Codex for one Screeps improvement and write what changed, what was accepted, and what was rejected.

sessions.name: Session 11: Colony Architecture and Demo Prep
sessions.objectives: Connect the Screeps bot pieces into a clear architecture: game interface, logic, Memory, APIs, automation, and version history.
sessions.liveActivity: Create a simple colony architecture diagram, clean the README, capture screenshots, and prepare the final walkthrough.
sessions.homework: Finish the final Screeps branch and prepare a 3-minute demo.

sessions.name: Session 12: Screeps Demo Day and Next Build
sessions.objectives: Present the working Screeps system, explain the architecture, review tradeoffs, and identify the next version.
sessions.liveActivity: Student Screeps demos, instructor feedback, peer questions, and a final reflection on programming, Git, APIs, automation, and AI.
sessions.homework: Submit final repo link, Screeps screenshots or demo clip, architecture notes, and reflection.

offerings.name: AI Systems Programming Lab - August Cohort
offerings.meetingFrequency: 6 weeks, Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern

product.title: AutoNateAI Student Demo Day
product.cta: The cohort ends with a live demo session where students show their Screeps colony, explain the system they built, the code decisions they made, and what they would automate next.

season.name: August Cohort Demo Day
season.winCondition: Students present a working Screeps bot, walk through the architecture, show their Git history, explain one automation or API/data flow, and reflect on what they would build next.
